programme.desc: Forbidden Science is a unique blend of noir science fiction and erotic programming. Set in the near future, the stories revolve around the evil corporation, 4Ever Innovations, whose young and brilliant scientists download and sell memories. They replace those we've lost with clones and devise technology that helps us live out our greatest fantasies; from saving a failed relationship, to creating the perfect lover, to being irresistible to the opposite sex - but sometimes our deepest desires come at a terrible price. 4Ever offers customers the opportunity to live out their fantasies in a virtual world but there are secrets within 4Ever Innovations that may cost you your life.

programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep112: Deep White - Why Sharks Attack
programme.desc: Deep White explores ways to research sharks to determine why sharks attack and to study innovative ways to train sharks in order to prevent future attacks. Sharks are surprisingly intelligent and able to perform complex tasks. Sharks can distinguish different signals and cues in feeding routines and they develop strong bonds with their trainers.

programme.title: The Great Barrier Reef: World's most amazing coral reef
programme.desc: This expedition to the Great Barrier Reef is designed to study the health and resilience of one of the world’s most amazing coral reefs. The expedition’s goal is to collect data about the world's most remote reefs and build a picture of how reefs are faring in today's changing climate.

programme.title: Next Stop Ep111: Tahiti
programme.desc: This Next Stop episode explores the three Tahitian Islands of Moorea, Huahine and Bora Bora via Quad rides, cultural hikes, jet skiing, exploring a Pearl Farm, shark & stingray feeding, and by checking out some of the best resorts as well as the local music scene.

programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep110: The Ship Sinkers
programme.desc: Mothballed, de-commissioned naval vessels are toxic time-bombs, filled with PCB’s, asbestos, lead paint, oil and grease. Hundreds rust in dockyards around the world. What can be done with them? A team of Canadians has developed an innovative solution…they clean the ships...then sink them!

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Episode 3 continues with a grueling desert run under Las Vegas’ heat, where endurance is tested to the extreme. As contestants push through, one unexpectedly stands out. They are then trained with cutting-edge weaponry, showcasing their skills on the course of fire. A high-stakes plank competition follows, with only two contestants left standing. Through intense physical and mental challenges, this episode reveals the contestants’ true character and resilience, setting the stage for more survival tests ahead.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: The best of the best are back to compete, and Don’s serving up a heaping plate of his signature 10-part PRT. A bear crawl down a mountain, log running and two-point planks are just some of Don’s welcome gauntlet. And the cherry on top—a 5k desert run. But the contestants only have themselves to blame. Because when you’re already amongst the elite, you have to do whatever it takes to find the one who stands above the rest.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: The CIA has called again, and Don has answered the call on behalf of the A-team. A team of special operatives are needed for a highly classified peace mission that could turn deadly at a moment's notice. To spearhead their mission training efforts, the team hits the range with some state-of-the-art SIRT pistols. Then, the gloves are off and the team gets their first course of fire for the season. After the fun course of fire, the team switches gears to knife throwing and fire building, with the help of Top Knives. It’s safe to say, these All-stars aren’t waiting a second.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The contestants of “Surviving Mann” are handpicked for their exceptional skills and determination. Under the guidance of survival expert Don Mann, they face a series of challenges designed to push them beyond their limits. Each contestant must adapt, innovate, and collaborate to survive in an unforgiving environment. As the grueling challenges unfold, they discover new strengths and forge deeper bonds. This episode sets the tone for a season filled with intense tests of endurance, strategy, and resilience, with Don Mann inspiring and pushing them every step of the way.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The stakes continue to climb as Green Light Sights rewards one top shooter with their very own custom weapon—proof that precision pays off. But there's no time to celebrate. Bear Arms ups the ante by issuing defense vests to every contestant, with one hard rule: once it’s on, it stays on. From grueling mission briefings to body armor plank challenges that push endurance to the brink, the weight—literally and mentally—never lets up. Still armored, the team reports to Ken Shamrock for another brutal dose of Lion’s Den physical punishment. Just when they think they’ve caught a break, Compliant Technologies’ infamous glove makes a shocking return to torment a fresh round of contestants. And as the day winds down, Don introduces the team to his personal “Flights” challenge—an unforgiving test no one is excited to take on. It all ends with a sobering CIA reminder: in the field, your backstory might just save your life—or end it.

With danger closing in, he must rely on his courage and quick thinking to bring the outlaws to justice and restore peace to the range

programme.title: The Outlaw 1943
programme.desc: Howard Hughes’ notorious Western, The Outlaw, ignites the screen with passion, rivalry, and danger. When outlaw Billy the Kid (Jack Buetel) rides into town, he finds himself entangled in a volatile triangle with legendary gunslinger Doc Holliday (Walter Huston) and ruthless lawman Pat Garrett (Thomas Mitchell). But it’s the fiery beauty Rio McDonald (Jane Russell, in her breakout role) who stirs the deadliest conflict of all. A tale of guns, desire, and betrayal, The Outlaw blazes with the daring spirit that made it one of Hollywood’s most talked-about films.

programme.title: Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Philippe travels to Pennsylvania to explore a hidden wonder beneath the Appalachian Mountains and see how researchers at Carnegie Mellon University are using cutting-edge science to measure air quality in Pittsburgh. We'll also investigate a bolder field where the rocks make a unique "ringing" sound and then stop by a mushroom farm to unearth how sustainable farming practices can positively impact inner city gardens.

programme.title: Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Philippe travels to Pennsylvania to explore a hidden wonder beneath the Appalachian Mountains and see how researchers at Carnegie Mellon University are using cutting-edge science to measure air quality in Pittsburgh. We'll also investigate a bolder field where the rocks make a unique "ringing" sound and then stop by a mushroom farm to unearth how sustainable farming practices can positively impact inner city gardens.

programme.title: Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Philippe travels to Pennsylvania to explore a hidden wonder beneath the Appalachian Mountains and see how researchers at Carnegie Mellon University are using cutting-edge science to measure air quality in Pittsburgh. We'll also investigate a bolder field where the rocks make a unique "ringing" sound and then stop by a mushroom farm to unearth how sustainable farming practices can positively impact inner city gardens.

programme.title: Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Philippe travels to Pennsylvania to explore a hidden wonder beneath the Appalachian Mountains and see how researchers at Carnegie Mellon University are using cutting-edge science to measure air quality in Pittsburgh. We'll also investigate a bolder field where the rocks make a unique "ringing" sound and then stop by a mushroom farm to unearth how sustainable farming practices can positively impact inner city gardens.

programme.title: Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Philippe travels to Pennsylvania to explore a hidden wonder beneath the Appalachian Mountains and see how researchers at Carnegie Mellon University are using cutting-edge science to measure air quality in Pittsburgh. We'll also investigate a bolder field where the rocks make a unique "ringing" sound and then stop by a mushroom farm to unearth how sustainable farming practices can positively impact inner city gardens.

programme.title: Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Philippe travels to Pennsylvania to explore a hidden wonder beneath the Appalachian Mountains and see how researchers at Carnegie Mellon University are using cutting-edge science to measure air quality in Pittsburgh. We'll also investigate a bolder field where the rocks make a unique "ringing" sound and then stop by a mushroom farm to unearth how sustainable farming practices can positively impact inner city gardens.
